Steve Vinter

← Recipes

Gochujang Tofu Bowl

Ingredients

  • 2 garlic cloves, peeled and minced (divided)
  • ½ tbsp gochujang 🌶
  • 2 tbsp peanut butter
  • 2 tsp tamari
  • 1 tsp turbinado sugar
  • 10 oz organic extra firm tofu, patted dry and cut into bite-size pieces
  • 6 oz green beans
  • 1 tbsp white sesame seeds
  • 8.8 oz precooked brown rice

From your kitchen

  • 1 tbsp + 1 tsp vegetable oil
  • Salt

Tools

  • Large nonstick skillet with lid

Directions

  1. Mix the gochujang sauce — Add just half the garlic, gochujang, just 1 tbsp peanut butter, tamari, turbinado sugar, and ¼ cup warm water to a medium bowl and mix the gochujang sauce.
  2. Crisp the tofu — Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add tofu and cook until crispy in places, 3 to 5 minutes. Add gochujang sauce and cook, stirring frequently, until crispy in places, 4 to 5 minutes. Transfer the gochujang tofu to a plate and cover to keep warm. Set aside the skillet for the next step.
  3. Make the green beans — Return the skillet to high heat and add 1 tsp vegetable oil, remaining garlic, green beans, sesame seeds, and a pinch of salt. Cook until garlic begins to brown, 2 to 3 minutes. Add ¼ cup water, cover, and cook until green beans are crisp-tender, 3 to 4 minutes.
  4. Make the rice — Make a small tear at the top of the brown rice bag and microwave bag for 1 minute.
  5. TIP: If you don't have a microwave, add precooked brown rice and 1 tbsp water to a small saucepan over medium-high heat. Cover and steam until warmed through, 3 to 4 minutes.
  6. Serve — Divide the brown rice between bowls and top with gochujang tofu and sesame garlic green beans. Dig in!

Nutrition

Nutrition per serving (as packaged) Calories: 580, Fat: 22 g, Sodium: 430 mg, Carbohydrates: 65 g, Protein: 35 g.
Allergens: peanut, sesame, soy. Manufactured on equipment shared with tree nuts, peanut, sesame, wheat, soy, egg, milk, fish, and shellfish.
Recipe from Purple Carrot.

Recipe from Purple Carrot.
